title: RepoMesh Overrides
description: Per-repo overrides for verifier behavior without forking verifier config.

allowlistAdd.description: Additional SPDX license IDs to allow for this repo

allowlistRemove.description: SPDX license IDs to disallow even if in global allowlist

treatUnknownAs.description: Override how unknown/missing licenses are treated

failOnSeverities.description: Severity levels that trigger security.scan fail. When present it UNIONS with (adds to) the threshold-derived fail floor {critical, high, unknown} — it can only ADD severities and can NEVER remove or downgrade critical, high, or unknown (D15 / SEC-001; enforced in verify-security computeFailSeverities). An empty array is rejected (minItems:1): under union semantics it is meaningless and historically read as a self-downgrade footgun. Omit the field entirely to keep just the severityThreshold-derived floor (which always includes critical, high, and unknown).

justification.description: Why this vuln is safe to ignore

ignoreVulns.description: Known-safe vulnerabilities to exclude from scoring

fail.description: A repo-level override cannot RAISE a failing bucket — fail is clamped to 0 (a failing check earns no assurance points). Raising fail/warn is profile/governance-ONLY; a repomesh.overrides.json that sets fail>0 would let a repo self-grant credit for a failing check (CRITICAL #2 / REG-002). warn must not exceed the profile/default warn for the check (enforced in build-trust resolveAssuranceWeights).
